Nexora Analytics // Mid Data Scientist

San Francisco, CA (Remote-Friendly) | Full-Time | $125,000 - $155,000 USD per annum

Transforming Business Insights Through Intelligent Data

>> ABOUT

Nexora Analytics is a mid-stage data science and analytics platform company founded in
2019. We partner with Fortune 500 companies and high-growth startups to unlock actionable

insights from their data. Our platform processes over 2 billion data points daily, enabling
customers across finance, e-commerce, and SaaS to make faster, data-driven decisions. We're

backed by Tier-1 VCs and operate with a mission to democratize advanced analytics for
organizations of all sizes.

>> SUMMARY

We're seeking a Mid Data Scientist to join our Analytics Platform team. You'll develop
predictive models, design and execute A/B tests, and build statistical analyses that drive

product and business decisions. This role offers the opportunity to work on complex, real-
world datasets and collaborate with product, engineering, and business teams. You should

have 2-5 years of professional data science experience and strong proficiency in Python,
SQL, and statistical methods.

>> RESPONSIBILITIES

- Develop and deploy machine learning models using scikit-learn and Python to solve
business problems, including feature engineering with Pandas and NumPy

- Design and analyze A/B tests and experiments to evaluate product changes; calculate
statistical significance and recommend actions based on results

- Write optimized SQL queries to extract, transform, and analyze data from multiple data
sources; collaborate with data engineering on pipeline improvements

- Conduct exploratory data analysis (EDA) and statistical modeling to uncover trends and
patterns in customer behavior and product usage

- Create reproducible Python scripts and Jupyter notebooks documenting methodology,
findings, and recommendations for stakeholders

- Collaborate with product managers and engineers to define KPIs, translate business
requirements into analytical requirements, and present findings to non-technical

audiences
- Build and maintain dashboards and automated reporting systems to track key metrics and

model performance over time
- Participate in code reviews, contribute to analytics best practices, and mentor junior

team members on statistical methods and modeling techniques

>> MUST HAVE

- 2-5 years of professional experience in data science, analytics, or a quantitative role

- Expert-level proficiency in Python, including libraries such as Pandas, NumPy, and
scikit-learn for data manipulation and modeling

- Strong SQL skills with ability to write complex queries for data extraction and
transformation from relational databases

 - Solid foundation in statistics and experimental design; experience designing and

interpreting A/B tests and statistical hypothesis tests
- Experience building, evaluating, and deploying machine learning models in production or

near-production environments
- Demonstrated ability to communicate technical findings clearly to both technical and

non-technical stakeholders

>> NICE TO HAVE

- Experience with causal inference methods or advanced experimental design techniques

- Familiarity with data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Looker, or similar)
- Knowledge of Python data visualization libraries (Matplotlib, Seaborn, or Plotly)

- Experience with version control (Git) and collaborative development practices

>> STACK

Python (Pandas, NumPy, scikit-learn) / SQL (PostgreSQL/MySQL) / Jupyter Notebooks / Statsmodels /

SciPy / A/B Testing & Experimentation Platforms / Tableau / Git/GitHub / AWS (S3, EC2, RDS) /
Apache Airflow

>> BENEFITS

- Competitive salary and equity package with early-stage company upside potential

- Comprehensive health insurance (medical, dental, vision) with employer contributions
- Professional development budget of $2,500 annually for courses, certifications, and

conferences
- Remote-flexible work arrangement with option to work from home 3+ days per week

- Unlimited PTO policy with a minimum of 15 days encouraged
- Collaboration with world-class data scientists and access to cutting-edge datasets

- Casual, inclusive work environment with a focus on continuous learning

>> APPLY

To apply, please submit your resume, a brief cover letter, and a link to your GitHub

profile or portfolio of relevant projects to careers@nexora.io. Optionally, include a link
to a published blog post, Kaggle profile, or writeup of a data science project. Screening

calls will begin immediately upon submission.
